results.title: A Progesterone Microneedle Patch for Self-Administration in the Prevention of Preterm Birth in a Mouse Model.
results.abstract: Progesterone, recommended for preventing preterm birth (PTB) in high-risk women, is traditionally administered via oral capsules, vaginal gels, and oil injections, which pose issues like low bioavailability, systemic side effects, and irritation, leading to reduced compliance. To address these issues, a user-friendly administration approach to deliver progesterone was needed to development for the prevention of PTB.
